docs.title: Home
docs.text: Visit Press kit to learn more about world's first open source widget creator.

docs.title: What is Sukko?
docs.text: Sukko is an open source Android app for creating widgets for home and lock screens.

docs.title: How to install
docs.text:
Latest version can be downloaded and installed from GitHub:

https://github.com/sadellie/sukko/releases/latest

or you can build the app yourself.

docs.title: Current stage
docs.text: Despite having most of the core features already implemented, the project is still in Experimental stage. Expect breaking changes until first full release.

docs.title: Developer contact
docs.text: Feel free to contact me. Prefer email.

docs.title: App name
docs.text:
Sukko

  • Do not change spelling
  • First letter should always be capitalized

docs.title: Logo
docs.text: Do not modify colors or symbols.

docs.title: Screenshots
docs.text: Can be slightly outdated, but they represent core functionality and main screens.

docs.title: Application
docs.text: The application doesn't collect or share any user data on its own.

docs.title: Third party
docs.text: The application allows you to access internet resources that you specify. Those internet resources have their own privacy policy that you should verify.

docs.title: Exported data
docs.text:
The application allows you to export and import user generated content. You are fully responsible for any information that it contains.

Open an issue in the repository or email me if you have questions.

docs.title: Scripting
docs.text:
In-app documentation

Methods, constants and templates are also available in the app. Try opening any script and click question mark (?) icon.

docs.title: Types
docs.text: Scripts support 3 types of values.

docs.title: Number
docs.text:
12
45.78
-90

docs.title: Text
docs.text: "texts must be in quotes"

docs.title: Boolean
docs.text:
true
false

docs.title: Declare a local variable
docs.text:
Local variables can make your script more readable and efficient.

Use = to declare a variable: 

x = 123

Local variables can be reassigned using same syntax: 

x = "new value"

You can use multiple variables. In the following example z will be 10: 

x = 2
y = 5
z = x * y

Variable types are dynamic

Same local variable can hold text, number or boolean

docs.title: Basic math
docs.text:
You can add, subtract, multiply and divide numbers.

  • Addition +
  • Substraction -
  • Multiplication *
  • Division /

Example:

2 + 2
5 - (-3)
8 * 456.123
99.999 / 3

Unary minus

Subtraction symbol can also be used to represent negative numbers

docs.title: Equality checks
docs.text:
Equality checks work only between same types.

Supported checks:

  • Equal ==
  • Not equal !=
  • Less <
  • Greater >
  • Less or equal <=
  • Greater or equal >=

Example:

(2 + 2) == 4
"text 1" != "text 2"

Brackets matter

Use brackets to to avoid unexpected issues with complex expressions

docs.title: Boolean operations
docs.text:
Boolean values can be combined to create a new value. This helps checking for multiple conditions.

Supported operations: - || – OR (disjunction) - && – AND (conjunction) - ! – NOT (negation)

Example: 

isMonday = ... // some boolean
isSunday = ... // some other boolean
if(isMonday || isSunday, "Weekend", "Not weekend")

This will return Weekend if either isMonday or isSunday is true.

docs.title: Battery status [TEXT]
docs.text:
Current battery status: CHARGING, DISCHARGING, FULL, NOT_CHARGING, UNKNOWN

batteryStatus

docs.title: Battery level [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Current battery level. From 0 to 100

batteryLevel

docs.title: Battery charge/discharge in seconds [NUMBER]
docs.text:
How many seconds left until battery charges to 100% or discharges to 0%

batteryFullEmpty

docs.title: Unix timestamp [NUMBER]
docs.text:
How many seconds passed since Jan 1st 1970

currentTimestamp

docs.title: Media artist [TEXT]
docs.text:
The artist of the last playing media. Can be empty.

mediaArtist

docs.title: Media title [TEXT]
docs.text:
The title of the last playing media. Can be empty.

mediaTitle

docs.title: Media playback duration [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Playback duration of the last playing media in seconds

mediaDuration

docs.title: Media playback position [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Playback position of the last playing media in seconds

mediaPosition

docs.title: Media cover URI [TEXT]
docs.text:
URI (link) for currently media cover of currently playing media. Can be empty.

mediaCover

docs.title: Media player name [TEXT]
docs.text:
Name of currently playing media source (app name). Can be empty.

playerName

docs.title: Media player icon [TEXT]
docs.text:
Uri to icon of currently playing media source (app logo). Can be empty.

playerIcon

docs.title: Media player state [TEXT]
docs.text:
Current state of player. Possible values: NONE, STOPPED, PAUSED, PLAYING, FAST_FORWARDING, REWINDING, BUFFERING, ERROR, SKIPPING_TO_PREVIOUS, SKIPPING_TO_NEXT, SKIPPING_TO_QUEUE_ITEM

playerState

docs.title: Device model [TEXT]
docs.text:
Current device model

deviceModel

docs.title: Minimal music volume [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Minimal volume of audio streams for music playback. Usually 0

volumeMusicMin

docs.title: Current music volume [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Current volume of audio streams for music playback

volumeMusic

docs.title: Max music volume [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Max volume of audio streams for music playback

volumeMusicMax

docs.title: Get global string [TEXT]
docs.text:
Get current value of a global string

globalString(id)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global string, see Globals tab in editor

docs.title: Get global number [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Get current value of a global number

globalNumber(id)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global number, see Globals tab in editor

docs.title: Get global boolean [BOOL]
docs.text:
Get current value of a global boolean

globalBoolean(id)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global boolean, see Globals tab in editor

docs.title: Set global number [NUMBER]
docs.text:
Set value for global number and return it. Doesn't change initial value.

setGlobalNumber(id, value)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global number, see Globals tab in editor
  • value [NUMBER] - New value

docs.title: Set global string [TEXT]
docs.text:
Set value for global text and return it. Doesn't change initial value.

setGlobalString(id, value)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global text, see Globals tab in editor
  • value [TEXT] - New value

docs.title: Set global boolean [BOOL]
docs.text:
Set value for global boolean and return it. Doesn't change initial value.

setGlobalBoolean(id, value)
  • id [NUMBER] - Id of a global boolean, see Globals tab in editor
  • value [BOOL] - New value

docs.title: Conditional check [TEXT, NUMBER, BOOL]
docs.text:
Methods accept parameters and return a new value

if (condition, ifTrue, ifFalse)
  • condition [BOOL] - condition expression
  • ifTrue [TEXT, NUMBER, BOOL] - returning value if condition is true
  • ifFalse [TEXT, NUMBER, BOOL] - returning value if condition is false

docs.title: Current date [TEXT]
docs.text:
Current formatted date

currentDate(format)
  • format [TEXT] - Formatting pattern

docs.title: Format timestamp [TEXT]
docs.text:
Format timestamp using specified pattern. Timestamp is converted to system time zone.

formatTimestamp(timestamp, format)
  • timestamp [TEXT] - timestamp in seconds
  • format [TEXT] - Formatting pattern

docs.title: Current date in time zone [TEXT]
docs.text:
Current formatted date in a specified time zone

currentDateWitTimeZone(format, timeZoneId)
  • format [TEXT] - Formatting pattern
  • timeZoneId [TEXT] - ID of a time zone

docs.title: Dynamic color [TEXT]
docs.text:
Extract a HEX value for a color from dynamic color scheme generated by system

dynamicColor(colorName)
  • colorName [TEXT] - Color token, case-insensitive. Allowed values: PRIMARY, ON_PRIMARY, PRIMARY_CONTAINER, ON_PRIMARY_CONTAINER, INVERSE_PRIMARY, SECONDARY, ON_SECONDARY, SECONDARY_CONTAINER, ON_SECONDARY_CONTAINER, TERTIARY, ON_TERTIARY, TERTIARY_CONTAINER, ON_TERTIARY_CONTAINER, BACKGROUND, ON_BACKGROUND, SURFACE, ON_SURFACE, SURFACE_VARIANT, ON_SURFACE_VARIANT, SURFACE_TINT, INVERSE_SURFACE, INVERSE_ON_SURFACE, ERROR, ON_ERROR, ERROR_CONTAINER, ON_ERROR_CONTAINER, OUTLINE, OUTLINE_VARIANT, SCRIM, SURFACE_BRIGHT, SURFACE_DIM, SURFACE_CONTAINER, SURFACE_CONTAINER_HIGH, SURFACE_CONTAINER_HIGHEST, SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOW, SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOWEST, PRIMARY_FIXED, PRIMARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_PRIMARY_FIXED, ON_PRIMARY_FIXED_VARIANT, SECONDARY_FIXED, SECONDARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_SECONDARY_FIXED, ON_SECONDARY_FIXED_VARIANT, TERTIARY_FIXED, TERTIARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_TERTIARY_FIXED, ON_TERTIARY_FIXED_VARIANT

docs.title: Color scheme from image [TEXT]
docs.text:
Extract a HEX value for a color from color scheme generated from image

colorScheme(colorName, source)
  • colorName [TEXT] - Color token, case-insensitive. Allowed values: PRIMARY, ON_PRIMARY, PRIMARY_CONTAINER, ON_PRIMARY_CONTAINER, INVERSE_PRIMARY, SECONDARY, ON_SECONDARY, SECONDARY_CONTAINER, ON_SECONDARY_CONTAINER, TERTIARY, ON_TERTIARY, TERTIARY_CONTAINER, ON_TERTIARY_CONTAINER, BACKGROUND, ON_BACKGROUND, SURFACE, ON_SURFACE, SURFACE_VARIANT, ON_SURFACE_VARIANT, SURFACE_TINT, INVERSE_SURFACE, INVERSE_ON_SURFACE, ERROR, ON_ERROR, ERROR_CONTAINER, ON_ERROR_CONTAINER, OUTLINE, OUTLINE_VARIANT, SCRIM, SURFACE_BRIGHT, SURFACE_DIM, SURFACE_CONTAINER, SURFACE_CONTAINER_HIGH, SURFACE_CONTAINER_HIGHEST, SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOW, SURFACE_CONTAINER_LOWEST, PRIMARY_FIXED, PRIMARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_PRIMARY_FIXED, ON_PRIMARY_FIXED_VARIANT, SECONDARY_FIXED, SECONDARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_SECONDARY_FIXED, ON_SECONDARY_FIXED_VARIANT, TERTIARY_FIXED, TERTIARY_FIXED_DIM, ON_TERTIARY_FIXED, ON_TERTIARY_FIXED_VARIANT
  • source [TEXT] - URI to image. Can be local (file://) or network (https://, http://) link

docs.title: Text based on battery status [TEXT]
docs.text: if (batteryStatus == "CHARGING", "Phone is charging", "Phone is not charging")

docs.title: Battery charge percentage [TEXT]
docs.text: batteryLevel"%"

docs.title: Text based battery charge [TEXT]
docs.text: if (batteryLevel == 100, "Battery is full", if(batteryLevel > 30, "Battery is ok", "Battery is low"))

docs.title: Current time [TEXT]
docs.text: currentDate("HH:mm")

docs.title: Time of device battery charge or discharge [TEXT]
docs.text: formatTimestamp(currentTimestamp + batteryFullEmpty, "HH:mm")

docs.title: Time in UTC [TEXT]
docs.text: currentDateWithTimeZone("HH:mm", "UTC")

docs.title: Primary dynamic color [TEXT]
docs.text: m3Color("PRIMARY")

docs.title: Outline dynamic color [TEXT]
docs.text: m3Color("OUTLINE")
